edges.description: Inherited common variants are modeled as upstream of brain molecular regulatory effects identified in ADHD multi-omics analyses.
Inherited common variants are modeled as upstream of methylation-mediated regulatory pathways identified in ADHD multi-omics analyses.
Brain gene-expression effects and alternative splicing effects are represented as parallel molecular regulatory consequences of ADHD genetic liability.
Molecular regulatory effects are represented as upstream contributors to neurodevelopmental circuit vulnerability.
DNA methylation-mediated effects are linked to ADHD through downstream expression or alternative-splicing processes.
Inefficient catecholamine signaling is modeled as directly weakening prefrontal cortical regulation of attention and behavior.
edges.source: Catecholaminergic Signaling Deficit in Prefrontal Cortex

edges.description: Prefrontal cortical weakness affects downstream basal ganglia and frontostriatal control loops.
Weak prefrontal networks impair top-down attention, behavioral inhibition, and working-memory control.
edges.target: Executive Function and Attention Regulation Impairment

edges.description: Altered frontostriatal regulation is represented as upstream of impaired executive control, attention regulation, and response inhibition.
edges.target: Executive Function and Attention Regulation Impairment

edges.description: Impaired executive and attentional regulation contributes to clinically observed inattention.
edges.source: Executive Function and Attention Regulation Impairment

edges.description: Impaired inhibitory control contributes to hyperactive and impulsive behavior.
edges.source: Executive Function and Attention Regulation Impairment

Catecholaminergic Signaling Deficit in Prefrontal Cortex
Catecholaminergic Signaling Deficit in Prefrontal Cortex

nodes.description: Alpha-2 agonist pharmacotherapy, especially guanfacine and clonidine, is modeled as targeting postsynaptic alpha-2A receptor signaling in prefrontal cortex to strengthen attention and behavioral regulation.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Guanfacine is currently used in both children and adults with ADHD. It has been shown to improve ratings on both the Inattention and Hyperactivity/Impulsivity scales, consistent with its widespread beneficial effects on many PFC functions.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Clonidine has a very rapid onset of action that can be helpful in treating emergent situations. However, it has significant sedative and hypotensive actions that limit its clinical utility.

nodes.description: Alternative splicing effects are modeled as a distinct molecular mechanism downstream of ADHD genetic liability and upstream of brain circuit vulnerability.

evidence.reference_title: A multi-omics study of brain tissue transcription and DNA methylation revealing the genetic pathogenesis of ADHD
evidence.snippet: We prioritized 966 unique genes that have statistically significant causal AS events, within at least one of the 14 different brain tissues.

nodes.description: Atomoxetine is a non-stimulant ADHD pharmacotherapy that increases prefrontal catecholamine availability by selectively blocking the norepinephrine transporter.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Effective pharmacologic treatments for ADHD all enhance catecholamine signaling in the PFC and strengthen its regulation of attention and behavior.

nodes.description: ADHD-associated genetic liability is linked to predicted causal effects on expression of many genes across brain tissues.

evidence.reference_title: A multi-omics study of brain tissue transcription and DNA methylation revealing the genetic pathogenesis of ADHD
evidence.snippet: Finally, we also prioritized the expression of 866 genes showing significant causal effects, including COMMD5, ENSG00000271904, HYAL3, etc., within at least one brain tissue.

nodes.description: Reduced or inefficient norepinephrine and dopamine signaling in the prefrontal cortex weakens top-down regulation of attention, behavior, and emotion.
nodes.id: Catecholaminergic Signaling Deficit in Prefrontal Cortex

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: The PFC requires optimal levels of norepinephrine (NE) and dopamine (DA) for proper functioning.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Genetic studies have consistently noted alterations in genes involved in catecholamine transmission in patients with ADHD.

nodes.description: DNA methylation-mediated regulatory pathways are modeled as epigenetic regulatory effects that influence ADHD through expression or splicing processes.

evidence.reference_title: A multi-omics study of brain tissue transcription and DNA methylation revealing the genetic pathogenesis of ADHD
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, through mediation analysis, 106 regulatory pathways were inferred where DNAm influences ADHD through gene expression or AS processes.

nodes.description: ADHD symptoms are linked to impaired executive functions, including top-down attention, response inhibition, and working-memory control.
nodes.id: Executive Function and Attention Regulation Impairment

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: The PFC is essential for the so-called executive functions, allowing us to organize and plan for the future and to inhibit responses to distractions in order to achieve a goal.

nodes.description: Dysregulation across prefrontal and striatal control loops is modeled as a circuit-level mechanism contributing to impaired inhibitory and attentional control.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: The PFC can guide behavioral output through its massive projections to the motor and premotor cortices, to basal ganglia structures such as the caudate and subthalamic nucleus, and to the cerebellum by way of the pons.

evidence.reference_title: White matter alterations in Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD): a systematic review of 129 diffusion imaging studies with meta-analysis
evidence.snippet: The systematic review highlighted white matter alterations (especially reduced FA) in projection, commissural and association pathways of individuals with ADHD, which were associated with symptom severity and cognitive deficits.

nodes.description: Excessive activity and impulsive behavior that are inappropriate for developmental level.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is characterized by symptoms of inattention, impulsivity, and locomotor hyperactivity.

nodes.description: Acting without adequate forethought or inhibitory control, represented as a distinct ADHD symptom domain alongside hyperactivity.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is characterized by symptoms of inattention, impulsivity, and locomotor hyperactivity.

nodes.description: Common-variant liability is transmitted from parents to offspring and contributes to ADHD trait risk. This node captures inherited upstream risk rather than a single-gene Mendelian mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic nurture versus genetic transmission of risk for ADHD traits in the Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study
evidence.snippet: This birth cohort study included 19,506 genotyped mother-father-offspring trios from the Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic nurture versus genetic transmission of risk for ADHD traits in the Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study
evidence.snippet: Our findings indicate that the intergenerational transmission of risk for ADHD traits is largely explained by the transmission of genetic variants from parents to offspring rather than by genetic nurture.

nodes.description: Weak structure, function, maturation, and connectivity of prefrontal cortex circuits reduce top-down control over attention and behavior.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Studies have found that ADHD is associated with weaker function and structure of prefrontal cortex (PFC) circuits, especially in the right hemisphere.

nodes.description: Reduced attention regulation with distractibility and difficulty sustaining attention.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is characterized by symptoms of inattention, impulsivity, and locomotor hyperactivity.

nodes.description: Stimulant medications such as methylphenidate and amphetamine strengthen prefrontal catecholamine signaling and are modeled as symptom-directed ADHD pharmacotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: The Emerging Neurobiology of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: The Key Role of the Prefrontal Association Cortex.
evidence.snippet: Effective pharmacologic treatments for ADHD all enhance catecholamine signaling in the PFC and strengthen its regulation of attention and behavior.
